column1: I have a clear goal in mind every time I open a social media app.
column2: I find myself scrolling through my phone without remembering why I picked it up.
column3: I consciously choose which digital platforms deserve my attention each day.
column4: My technology use is driven by my needs rather than by notifications.
column5: I find it easy to put my phone in another room when I need to focus.
column6: I feel a strong urge to check my device the moment it vibrates or pings.
column7: I successfully set 'tech-free' zones or times during my daily routine.
column8: I struggle to stop using my devices even when I know I should be sleeping.
column9: I feel that my online personality is a true reflection of who I am offline.
column10: I often post things online mainly to seek validation from others.
column11: My digital interactions leave me feeling more connected rather than more lonely.
column12: I find myself comparing my life unfavorably to the 'highlight reels' of others online.
column13: I can see photos of events I wasn't invited to without feeling upset.
column14: I feel anxious or 'out of the loop' if I am away from my phone for more than an hour.
column15: I am able to ignore online negativity or 'trolling' without it ruining my mood.
